title: Graduation Advice for a Changing Job Market

source.title: Noteworthy speeches from recent college graduations

segments.title: A New Chapter
segments.audioText: College seniors have been donning their cap and gowns to mark the end of one chapter and the beginning of another.
segments.transcript: College seniors have been donning their cap and gowns to mark the end of one chapter and the beginning of another.
segments.translationZh: 大學畢業生一直戴上畢業帽、著上畢業袍，標誌住一個階段結束，另一個階段開始。

points.label: College seniors
points.explanationZh: 指大學最後一年學生；新聞入面 seniors 唔一定係長者，要睇上下文。

points.label: have been donning
points.explanationZh: donning 係較正式嘅『穿上、戴上』；have been 加 ing 表示最近一直發生。

points.label: the end of one chapter
points.explanationZh: chapter 喺呢度係人生階段，唔係書本章節；係好常見嘅比喻。

segments.title: A Daunting Future
segments.audioText: They're facing a daunting future, entering a rapidly transforming job market, thanks to artificial intelligence, global unrest and a host of other factors.
segments.transcript: They're facing a daunting future, entering a rapidly transforming job market, thanks to artificial intelligence, global unrest and a host of other factors.
segments.translationZh: 佢哋面對一個令人有壓力嘅未來，進入一個因人工智能、全球動盪同其他好多因素而快速轉變嘅就業市場。

points.label: facing a daunting future
points.explanationZh: daunting 指令人卻步、有壓力；聽到 facing 就知道係『面對』一個情況。

points.label: rapidly transforming job market
points.explanationZh: rapidly 係快速；transforming job market 即就業市場正急速改變。

points.label: a host of other factors
points.explanationZh: a host of 係『好多』；新聞常用嚟包住一串未逐一列出嘅原因。

segments.title: Best Advice
segments.audioText: It was against this backdrop that graduation speakers took to the stage to give their best advice.
segments.transcript: It was against this backdrop that graduation speakers took to the stage to give their best advice.
segments.translationZh: 正係喺呢個背景之下，畢業典禮講者走上台，分享佢哋最好嘅建議。

points.label: against this backdrop
points.explanationZh: 意思係『喺呢個背景之下』，新聞好常用嚟連接大環境同具體事件。

points.label: took to the stage
points.explanationZh: 即係上台；took to 聽起來係一個完整片語，唔好逐字拆得太散。

points.label: give their best advice
points.explanationZh: best advice 係最想畀人嘅提醒或建議，口語同正式場合都常見。

segments.title: Fear and Confidence
segments.audioText: Overcoming fear and doubt in the face of those challenges is where you're going to gain the confidence to make your best choices.
segments.transcript: Overcoming fear and doubt in the face of those challenges is where you're going to gain the confidence to make your best choices.
segments.translationZh: 喺面對嗰啲挑戰時克服恐懼同懷疑，正正係你會建立信心、作出最好選擇嘅地方。

points.label: Overcoming fear and doubt
points.explanationZh: overcoming 係克服；fear and doubt 成對出現，意思好清楚。

points.label: in the face of those challenges
points.explanationZh: 固定講法，意思係面對那些挑戰時；in the face of 連住聽。

points.label: gain the confidence
points.explanationZh: gain 可以解建立、得到；confidence 係信心。

segments.title: Keep Community Close
segments.audioText: Success is building a community and keeping that community close.
segments.transcript: Success is building a community and keeping that community close.
segments.translationZh: 成功係建立一個社群，並且同呢個社群保持緊密連繫。

points.label: Success is building
points.explanationZh: Success is + ing 係定義式講法：成功係做某件事。

points.label: a community
points.explanationZh: community 可以係朋友、同事、支持你嘅人，唔一定係正式組織。

points.label: keeping that community close
points.explanationZh: keep someone close 即保持關係親近；close 呢度係形容關係。

sentences: College seniors have been donning their cap and gowns to mark the end of one chapter and the beginning of another.
They're facing a daunting future, entering a rapidly transforming job market, thanks to artificial intelligence, global unrest and a host of other factors.
It was against this backdrop that graduation speakers took to the stage to give their best advice.
Overcoming fear and doubt in the face of those challenges is where you're going to gain the confidence to make your best choices.
Success is building a community and keeping that community close.

vocabulary.meaningZh: 大學或高中最後一年學生；高年級生
vocabulary.example: College seniors are preparing to graduate.
vocabulary.translationZh: 大學最後一年學生正準備畢業。

vocabulary.example: Students were donning their cap and gowns.
vocabulary.translationZh: 學生正戴上畢業帽、著上畢業袍。

vocabulary.example: They are facing a daunting future.
vocabulary.translationZh: 佢哋面對一個令人有壓力嘅未來。

vocabulary.example: AI is transforming the job market.
vocabulary.translationZh: 人工智能正改變就業市場。

vocabulary.example: The speeches happened against this backdrop.
vocabulary.translationZh: 演講係喺呢個背景之下發生。

vocabulary.example: You can gain confidence by preparing well.
vocabulary.translationZh: 你好好準備，就可以建立信心。

phrases.phrase: mark the end of one chapter
phrases.example: Graduation can mark the end of one chapter.

phrases.meaningZh: 一個快速轉變嘅就業市場
phrases.example: Graduates are entering a rapidly transforming job market.
Against this backdrop, speakers gave their best advice.

phrases.phrase: in the face of those challenges
phrases.example: Confidence grows in the face of those challenges.

phrases.meaningZh: 同嗰個社群保持緊密連繫
phrases.example: Success is keeping that community close.

speakingPractice.instructionZh: 由 00:00 跟讀第一句，留意 end of one chapter / beginning of another 呢組對比。
重播 00:12，先聽 rapidly transforming job market，再補返 thanks to artificial intelligence。
模仿 01:06 嘅句式，用 Overcoming fear and doubt 開頭講一次。
用兩句英文複述：Graduates face a changing job market. Speakers advised them to build confidence and community.

notes: 今日重點係畢業同職場轉變嘅新聞語氣，句子較正式，但都好適合練 chunking。
先用時間碼盲聽，再對照 KQED/PBS 頁面嘅 CC transcript；唔需要改寫原句嚟背。
第二句資訊多，可以拆成 facing a daunting future / entering a job market / thanks to... 三段。
dailyLifePractice 會延伸到求職、請人畀意見同簡短工作更新，保持輕鬆實用。

dailyLifePractice.title: Asking for Career Advice
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你想請同事或朋友畀少少職涯建議，語氣自然、有禮貌。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Could I ask you for some quick career advice? I'm trying to figure out my next step. What helped you when you were starting out?

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Could I ask you for some quick career advice?
lines.zh: 可唔可以請你畀我少少職涯建議？

lines.speaker: Coworker
lines.en: Of course. What's on your mind?

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I'm trying to figure out my next step.
lines.zh: 我想搞清楚下一步應該點行。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: What helped you when you were starting out?
lines.zh: 你啱啱開始嗰陣，有咩幫到你？

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: Could I ask you for... 比 Can you give me... 柔和自然。What's on your mind? 係美國人好常用嘅開放式回應。

drills.label: Substitution
drills.prompt: 將 career advice 換成 feedback、restaurant tips 或 travel advice。
drills.example: Could I ask you for some quick feedback?

drills.label: Role-play
drills.prompt: 你問第一句，然後用 I'm trying to figure out... 補充你嘅情況。
drills.example: I'm trying to figure out my schedule for next week.

dailyLifePractice.title: Quick Job Search Update
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：朋友問你搵工進展，你用簡短自然英文交代近況。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: It's been a little challenging, but I'm making progress. I sent out a few applications this week. I'm also trying to build my network.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: It's been a little challenging, but I'm making progress.
lines.zh: 係有少少挑戰，不過我有進展。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I sent out a few applications this week.
lines.zh: 我今個星期 send 咗幾份申請。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I'm also trying to build my network.
lines.zh: 我亦都試緊建立自己嘅人脈。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: It's been a little challenging 聽落誠實但唔太負面。making progress 係好自然嘅正面補充。

drills.label: Quick repeat
drills.prompt: 跟讀 It's been a little challenging, but I'm making progress 三次。
drills.example: It's been a little challenging, but I'm making progress.

drills.label: Say-it-your-way
drills.prompt: 用 I sent out a few... 講一個你最近完成嘅小任務。
drills.example: I sent out a few emails this morning.

dailyLifePractice.title: Small Talk After a Big Change
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你同鄰居或同事閒聊近況，講人生新階段但唔太正式。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: I'm starting a new chapter, so I'm a little nervous. But I'm excited too. I'm just taking it one step at a time.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I'm starting a new chapter, so I'm a little nervous.
lines.zh: 我開始一個新階段，所以有少少緊張。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I'm just taking it one step at a time.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: starting a new chapter 係自然但有少少感性嘅講法。one step at a time 好適合講轉工、搬屋、返學等新開始。

drills.label: Polite response
drills.prompt: 用 I'm a little nervous, but... 講一句平衡感受。
drills.example: I'm a little nervous, but I'm excited too.

drills.label: Substitution
drills.prompt: 將 new chapter 換成 new job、new class 或 new city。
drills.example: I'm starting a new job, so I'm a little nervous.
